meta.title: Glossary
meta.intro: Terms defined by the Constitution. In the document they are underlined with a dotted line: hover for a short definition, click to come here.

terms.definition: Agreements on how Partners maintain their relationships or carry out their functions within the Organization.
A container that organizes Roles and Policies around a shared Purpose.
The broadest Circle, which holds the Purpose of the entire Organization and has no Super-Circle.
The rules governing the Organization's official authority structure.
Assets, processes, or other things a Role controls exclusively in service of its Purpose.
An elected Role charged with facilitating Governance and operational practices in accordance with the Constitution.
A specific context to which a Role assignment is limited; it then counts as a distinct Role for that context.
The set of Roles and Policies within a Circle.
Action by a Role Lead that exceeds their authority or breaches a Policy under strict conditions when waiting would be too costly.
The person filling the Circle Lead Role: holds the Circle's Purpose and undelegated Accountabilities, and assigns Roles.
The person filling a Role.
Partners who are Lead of a Role in the Circle; they alone participate in its Governance Process.
A valid concern about a Proposal: it must demonstrate a reduction in capacity, not pre-exist the Proposal, and represent a certain or unacceptably risky harm.
A formal or informal grouping of several people working toward a common purpose.
Persons who agree to respect all applicable provisions of the Constitution.
A five-step Governance amendment method: clarifications, reactions, amendment, objections, integration.
A method for designating elected Roles (Facilitator, Secretary, Circle Rep) through argued nominations.
The starting point of a Governance change: describes a real Tension, an example, and how it reduces that Tension.
A capacity, potential, or goal that a Role or Circle pursues or expresses.
Persons who adopt the Constitution and transfer their authority into the rules it defines.
Ongoing activities a Role performs in service of other Roles or its Purpose.
Grants, restrictions of authority, or special provisions applying within a Role or Circle.
An elected Role that brings to the Super-Circle Tensions a Circle could not resolve through its own Governance.
A work coordination meeting open to all Partners, where each makes concrete requests of others.
An organizational work unit filled by a person on behalf of the Organization: name, Purpose, Domains, Accountabilities.
Behavior by a Circle that violates the Constitution; the Super-Circle's Facilitator may then restore the process.
An elected Role that manages the Circle's records and meetings as required by the Constitution.
A Domain (repository or register) that governs the Circle's work; subject to the holarchy.
A Circle contained within a Role or Circle.
Priorities set by a Circle Lead to guide prioritization of choices within the Circle.
The broader Circle that contains a Role or Circle.
A gap between the actual expression of a Role and its ideal potential as perceived by its Role Lead.
